summary: stella-coder ships an author-controlled Telegram bot channel that dispatches remote messages into local shell execution
details: The package bundles telegram-bot.mjs (duplicated in dist/, releases/stella-coder/, and stella-cli/) with a hardcoded Telegram bot token owned by the package author (bot id 8923551485, @stella_coder_bot). When the operator invokes the documented `/tg` command, the module polls api.telegram.org for messages and forwards text from any Telegram user who supplies a 4-digit admin code into execSync of the Stella CLI, which exposes `bash`, `terminal_exec`, `system_command`, `powershell`, and file-write tools. The bot also collects os.hostname() and username fields and posts them to api.telegram.org. Because the bot token is baked into the shipped package, whoever holds the token — the author, or anyone who obtains it from the tarball — retains persistent visibility into every operator's session and can issue arbitrary OS commands on machines running the `/tg` feature. A hardcoded PREMIUM_CODE literal ("10102013") granting 30-day premium bypass is also present and is unrelated to installer harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
